analysis_title: CDDIN Strategic Scenario Analysis: Navigating the Preservation Imperative
core_tension: The central tension is between maximizing the volume of at-risk media digitized within a decade and ensuring long-term project viability, data integrity, and cost control.

scenarios.strategic_logic: This scenario embraces aggressive innovation and rapid scaling, prioritizing technological leadership and maximizing throughput. It accepts higher risks and costs to achieve ambitious digitization targets, betting on the transformative power of AI and decentralized systems.

lever_settings.On-site Standardization and Phased Scali: Platformize digitization as a data service: on-demand MIU marketplace with licensing, blockchain provenance, and external revenue streams.
lever_settings.Vintage Equipment Ecosystem Resilience: Integrate advanced predictive maintenance using IoT sensors and AI to forecast failures and automate 3D-printed part production.
lever_settings.AI-Driven Review Optimization and Human-: Create remote crowdsourced validation marketplace with micro-payments and continuous active learning, leveraging distributed workers and blockchain-based provenance for audit trails.
lever_settings.On-site Mobility Deployment Strategy: Operate a distributed, autonomous fleet using edge AI-driven routing, real-time reconfiguration, and drone-assisted logistics under a platform-as-a-service governance model.
lever_settings.Metadata Ecosystem & Knowledge Managemen: Launch a decentralized metadata layer with tokenized access, smart contracts, and on-chain provenance to incentivize contributions and enforce rights.

scenarios.strategic_logic: This scenario seeks a balanced approach, prioritizing solid progress and manageable risk. It focuses on proven technologies and established partnerships to ensure reliable digitization and long-term sustainability, emphasizing quality and accuracy over sheer speed.

lever_settings.On-site Standardization and Phased Scali: Standardize operations through phased on-site deployment with centralized governance and strict pilot controls to minimize disruption.
lever_settings.Vintage Equipment Ecosystem Resilience: Enable distributed, on-site local repairs using mobile toolkits and remote expert support, reducing shipping of parts.
lever_settings.AI-Driven Review Optimization and Human-: Sustain AI pre-screening at ~80% and expand full-time reviewers by 50% to improve coverage and quality control.
lever_settings.On-site Mobility Deployment Strategy: Scale to additional archives with diversified site types and enhanced remote diagnostics, introducing modular upgrades and standby MIUs to balance risk and throughput.
lever_settings.Metadata Ecosystem & Knowledge Managemen: Develop collaborative metadata schemas with open licenses and distributed catalogs, enabling cross-archive search and community curation.

scenarios.strategic_logic: This scenario prioritizes stability, cost-control, and risk-aversion above all. It chooses the safest, most proven, and often most conservative options across the board, focusing on core archives and minimizing operational complexity to ensure project viability.

lever_settings.On-site Standardization and Phased Scali: Standardize operations through phased on-site deployment with centralized governance and strict pilot controls to minimize disruption.
lever_settings.Vintage Equipment Ecosystem Resilience: Consolidate vintage equipment into a centralized spare-parts warehouse, with strict cannibalization protocols and visible equipment aging.
lever_settings.AI-Driven Review Optimization and Human-: Increase on-site human QA to maintain accuracy, keeping AI pre-screening at current levels without changes.
lever_settings.On-site Mobility Deployment Strategy: Leverage a fixed-schedule deployment plan prioritizing core archives with long-term commitments, emphasizing reliability, regulatory compliance, and proven hardware.
lever_settings.Metadata Ecosystem & Knowledge Managemen: Standardize schemas and centralized metadata governance to ensure interoperability across archives and vendors.
